description: Russian language pack — pilot scope. 71 vocabulary items (68 words + 3 phrases), 9 grammar points (with one substitution ladder), 9 Cyrillic-literacy pronunciation rules, 3 dialogues, all A1. Built to validate the third-language / new-script case end-to-end (Cyrillic display, transliteration, case-system content) before investing in full Tier-1 volume, the same pattern used to pilot pt-br and ja.
coverage: A1 everyday basics: greetings, self-introduction, numbers, time, family, food/drink, core verbs, adjectives, question words, and the grammar most load-bearing at this level — noun gender, the absence of a present-tense 'to be', an overview of the six-case system, the prepositional case (location) and genitive-after-negation (нет), first/second conjugation present tense, adjective-gender agreement, and intonation-only yes/no questions. Pronunciation content is deliberately alphabet-literacy-first (Latin-lookalike false-friend letters, hard/soft signs, iotated vowels, ы vs и, vowel reduction/akanye, unmarked word stress, final devoicing, the -ого/-его exception) since a learner can't use anything else in the pack without being able to read Cyrillic. Deliberately excludes slang/profanity and the remaining four cases (genitive beyond negation, dative, accusative, instrumental) — those are natural next steps once the pilot is reviewed, not oversights.
editorialNotes: Cyrillic text and transliteration were authored directly (no machine translation pass). Every vocabulary/dialogue item carries both the Cyrillic form (lemma/text) and a stress-marked Latin transliteration (romaji field) side by side, per an explicit product requirement that Cyrillic and a phonetic/latinized rendering appear together everywhere text appears — the same reading+romaji pattern the app already uses for Japanese, just without a separate 'reading' layer since Cyrillic doesn't need one. Stress marks (´) in the romaji are a deliberate authoring choice, not standard Russian orthography, since so much pronunciation (vowel reduction, akanye) depends on knowing which syllable is stressed and normal Russian text doesn't mark it. See NATIVE_REVIEW.md.
